title: Validate model IDs before shell invocation to prevent injection
context: Model IDs from HUNCH_*_MODEL env vars become argv tokens in a Windows cmd.exe line (shell:true). Untrusted commit/diff content is already stdin-only, so these env vars are the lone non-literal token reaching the shell—a poisoned value could smuggle commands like `& evil.exe`. Touches invariant(s): con_2ce3f2a547 (Synthesis must run on the Claude subscription, never the pay-per-token API: strip ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from the spawned env).
decision: Add `safeModel()` function: whitelist-validate model IDs against regex `/^[A-Za-z0-9._:/-]+$/`, reject any whitespace or cmd.exe metacharacters, and fall back to the provider's default instead of crashing.
consequences: Closes the only shell-injection attack surface on Windows
Typos in HUNCH_*_MODEL env vars silently fall back to defaults instead of crashing
Model ID pattern is now constrained to alphanumeric, dots, underscores, colons, forward/backslashes
Adds 3 test cases covering valid ids, injection attempts, and missing/empty env vars
alternatives_rejected: Reject and crash on invalid model ID (worse UX than fallback)
Quote or escape argv tokens (cmd.exe line-join escaping is unreliable)
Route model ID through stdin instead (breaks provider API contracts)
Remove env var override capability (loses flexibility for custom/test models)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Reject and crash on invalid model ID (worse UX than fallback)
